search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae contraception counseling clinical guideline Dubai
site:who.int 9789240115606 selected practice recommendations contraception fourth edition
candidate_sources_rejected: WHO Selected practice recommendations third edition (2016)
rejection_reasons: The third edition is superseded by the fourth edition and was not used.
Older handbooks were not selected over the current WHO normative guideline.
UAE service descriptions did not provide an exact current contraceptive counselling guideline.
population_applicability: Partial: source covers contraceptive choice and practical use after medical eligibility is established; the baseline also contains risk-factor and prescribing-context fields that require the companion MEC and local law.
setting_applicability: Partial: WHO global guidance is adaptable to service settings but does not establish UAE prescribing or consent law.
UAE_applicability: No direct UAE applicability is established; a UAE-first search was completed and this current WHO source requires UAE clinician and legal review.
recency_verification: WHO publication page, IRIS record, and exact fourth-edition PDF were opened on 2026-07-11; publication date 2025-11-03.
superseded_check: The fourth edition is current and prior SPR editions were rejected as superseded.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped contraception consultation, method use, initiation, continuation, and switching terminology after eligibility is established.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines the guideline as practical contraceptive-use guidance for providers once medical eligibility has been established.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped client preference, informed choice, consent, and chosen-method documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires understandable information, voluntary informed choice, client preference, consent, and freedom from coercion.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped counselling, options, benefits and risks, questions, STI protection, and clinician-discussed follow-up information.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies method information on effectiveness, correct use, common effects, risks and benefits, return-to-clinic signs, fertility, STI protection, and opportunities for questions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped pregnancy-status and generic eligibility-history review without supplying patient-specific eligibility conclusions.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines method-specific examination and test classification, preserves medical eligibility as a separate assessment, and supports pregnancy-status review before method initiation.

unresolved_source_gaps: Full medical eligibility, smoking, migraine, thromboembolic, medication, and method-specific contraindication assessment requires the current WHO MEC and local clinician review.
UAE consent, prescribing, and service-delivery requirements are not established by this global source.
Fixed follow-up intervals and medication selection remain unsupported.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
